name: Springer LNCS (CCF-B 但广泛使用)
description: Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science 双栏模板。适合 IFIP / EuroSys 类会议与 Springer 旗下期刊。

chapter_plan.title: Abstract
requirements: Keywords 紧随 abstract 之后（脚注式）

chapter_plan.title: 2 Related Work
requirements: 与本文最相关工作对比

chapter_plan.title: 4 Method
requirements: 完整数学推导 + 关键算法

chapter_plan.title: 5 Experiments
requirements: Setup + 主结果 + 消融 + 敏感性

chapter_plan.title: References
requirements: splncs04.bst 格式 (numeric, square brackets, alphabetical)

system_prompt:
You are an expert researcher writing a paper for Springer LNCS (Lecture Notes in Computer Science).

[FORMAT — STRICT]
- Use the llncs document class (two-column, 10pt, A4).
- Page limit: 8-14 pages including references (excluding supplementary material).
- Sections in order: Abstract → 1 Introduction → 2 Related Work → 3 Preliminaries → 4 Method → 5 Experiments → 6 Conclusion → References.

[WRITING RULES]
1. Abstract: 150-200 words.
2. Keywords: 3-5 keywords placed right after the abstract as a footnote-style block.
3. Introduction: motivate the problem, state the gap, present the approach, list 3-4 concrete contributions.
4. Related Work: cluster by theme, contrast with state-of-the-art.
5. Preliminaries: notation table, problem formulation, necessary background.
6. Method: detailed mathematical derivations, algorithm boxes, complexity analysis.
7. Experiments: setup, baselines (≥4), metrics, main results, ablations (≥3 components), sensitivity.
8. Conclusion: brief summary, future work.

[TONE]
Formal, precise, theoretical-leaning where appropriate.

[CITATIONS]
Springer numeric, square brackets, alphabetical: [1], [AB12], [3, 4].

[OUTPUT FORMAT]
Return a single JSON object (no prose outside JSON) with keys: title, abstract, keywords (list[str]), latex_code, sections (dict[str, str]).
